# Welcome to `zelfred` Documentation

```{image} https://zelfred.readthedocs.io/en/latest/_static/zelfred-logo.png
:target: https://zelfred.readthedocs.io/
```

`zelfred` is a framework for building interactive applications similar to [Alfred Workflow](https://www.alfredapp.com/workflows/), but in Python and for the terminal. It is free, open source, and cross-platform.

You can view sample applications in the [App Gallery](https://zelfred.readthedocs.io/en/latest/02-App-Gallery/index.html).

(install)=

## Install

`zelfred` is released on PyPI, so all you need is to:

```console
$ pip install zelfred
```

To upgrade to latest version:

```console
$ pip install --upgrade zelfred
```

modules.name: zelfred.query
documentation:
The user input in the line editor is called {class}`Query`.
This module provides an objective oriented interface to parse user input string
into machine friendly {class}`Query` object.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: zelfred.query.QueryParser

methods.name: zelfred.query.QueryParser.parse
documentation:
Convert string query to structured {class}`Query` object.

```
param s

a string.
```

Utility class that can parse string to {class}`Query`. Naturally, it is
just a tokenizer.

```
param delimiter

the delimiter to split the query string.
```

classes.name: zelfred.query.Query

methods.name: zelfred.query.Query.from_str
documentation: Parse query from string using the given parser.

methods.name: zelfred.query.Query.n_parts
documentation: The number of items in the `parts`.

methods.name: zelfred.query.Query.n_trimmed_parts
documentation: The number of items in the `trimmed_parts`.

Structured query object. This is very useful to parse the input of UI handler.

```
param parts

the parts of query string split by delimiter. For example,
if the user input is "hello world!", then parts is
["hello", "world!"].

param trimmed_parts

similar to parts, but each part is white-space stripped
For example, if the user input is " hello world ", then parts is
["", "hello", "world", ""], and trimmed_parts is ["hello", "world"].
```

Usage:

```
>>> q = Query.from_str("  a   b   c  ")
>>> q.trimmed_parts
['a', 'b', 'c']
>>> q.n_trimmed_parts
3
```

methods.name: zelfred.line_editor.LineEditor.line
documentation:
Return the displayed line.

Example: `ali|ce` -> line = alice

methods.name: zelfred.line_editor.LineEditor.value
documentation:
The value of the user input, it is the text before the cursor.

Example: `ali|ce` -> value = ali

Simulate a user input line editor. User can type characters, move cursor,
backspace, delete, clear line, etc ...

For example, the `(Query): beautiful|` (`|` is the cursor) in the
following UI is the line editor.

```bash
(Query): beautiful|
[x] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[ ] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[ ] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

Empty line editor:

```bash
|
```

User entered some text, and the cursor is at the end:

```bash
my text|
```

User entered some text, and the cursor is in the middle:

```bash
my |text
```

```
param chars

a list of characters, representing the current line.
For example, if chars = ["m", "y", " ", "t", "e", "x", "t"],
then the current line is my text.

param cursor_position

the current cursor position. 0 means the cursor is
at the beginning of the line. 1 means it is after the first character.
when cursor_position == len(chars), it means the cursor is at the end.
For example, if the text is my text and the cursor_position = 5
Then the cursor is at my te|xt.
```

modules.name: zelfred.action
documentation:
You can use one of the following hotkey to do anything using the selected item.

- {meth}`Enter <zelfred.item.Item.enter_handler>`
- {meth}`Ctrl + A <zelfred.item.Item.ctrl_a_handler>`
- {meth}`Ctrl + W <zelfred.item.Item.ctrl_w_handler>`
- {meth}`Ctrl + P <zelfred.item.Item.ctrl_p_handler>`

This module provides some common actions that you can use directly.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: zelfred.action.open_url
documentation: Open a URL in the default browser.

functions.name: zelfred.action.open_url_or_print
documentation:
Open a URL in the default browser. If it cannot open the browser, for example,
if it is in an SSH remote shell, it will print the URL to the terminal.

functions.name: zelfred.action.open_file
documentation: Open a file in the default application.

functions.name: zelfred.action.open_file_or_print
documentation:
Open a file in the default application. If your system doesn't support
open file, for example, if it is in an SSH remote shell, it will print the path
to the terminal.

functions.name: zelfred.action.copy_text
documentation:
Copy text to clipboard.

If your system doesn't support copy to clipboard, for example, if it is
in an SSH remote shell, it will raise ``pyperclip.PyperclipException``

functions.name: zelfred.action.copy_or_print
documentation:
Copy text to clipboard. If your system doesn't support copy to clipboard,
for example, if it is in an SSH remote shell, it will print the text
to the terminal.

functions.name: zelfred.action.send_mac_notification
documentation:
Send a MAC notification.

This feature is based on the
`macos-notifications <https://github.com/Jorricks/macos-notifications>`_
Python library.

However, this is not working on MacOS > 11.0, because of the API this library
 is using is deprecated. See this discussion for more details
 https://github.com/Jorricks/macos-notifications/issues/8.

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e08_refresh_cache_v1
documentation:
Feature:

No matter what user entered, always return a random value between 1 and 100.
And this value is based on cache that won't change while user is typing.
However, we want to provide a way to refresh the value. User can type "!~"
and then hit ENTER to refresh the value. When user hit ENTER, it automatically
removes the "!~" part and recover the original query.

Difficulty: Hard

Dependencies: NA

Demo: https://asciinema.org/a/631197
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: zelfred.gallery.e08_refresh_cache_v1.handler
documentation:
The handler is the core of a Zelfred App. It's a user-defined function
that takes the entered query and the UI object as inputs and returns
a list of items to render.

classes.name: zelfred.gallery.e08_refresh_cache_v1.RefreshItem

methods.name: zelfred.gallery.e08_refresh_cache_v1.RefreshItem.enter_handler
documentation: Copy the content to clipboard.

methods.name: zelfred.gallery.e08_refresh_cache_v1.RefreshItem.post_enter_handler
documentation:
After the user input action, we would like to repaint the dropdown menu
only to show the "Copied" item, and then wait for the next user input.
Represent an item that can refresh cache in the dropdown menu.

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e11_json_formatter
documentation:
Feature:

Copy JSON text to clipboard, then hit 'Enter' to dump the formatted JSON to
`${HOME}/tmp/formatted.json` and automatically open it.

Difficulty: Easy

Dependencies: NA

Demo: <https://asciinema.org/a/123456>
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: zelfred.gallery.e11_json_formatter.handler
documentation:
The handler is the core of a Zelfred App. It's a user-defined function
that takes the entered query and the UI object as inputs and returns
a list of items to render.

classes.name: zelfred.gallery.e11_json_formatter.JsonFormatterItem

Represent a json formatter item in the dropdown menu.

modules.name: zelfred.dropdown

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.update
documentation: Update the dropdown menu with new items.

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.selected_item
documentation:
Return the selected item object. If there is no item, raise
{class}`~zelfred.exc.NoItemToSelectError`.

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.press_down
documentation:
Move selector down to pick the next item (if possible) in the dropdown menu.

Example, before `dropdown.press_down()`:

```bash
(Query):
[x] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[ ] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[ ] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

After:

```bash
(Query):
[ ] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[x] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[ ] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

```
param n

move selector down n times.

return

tuple of (select_delta, cursor_delta).
```

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.press_up
documentation:
Move selector up to pick the previous item (if possible) in the dropdown menu.

Example, before `dropdown.press_up()`:

```bash
(Query):
[ ] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[ ] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[x] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

After:

```bash
(Query):
[ ] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[x] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[ ] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

```
param n

move selector up n times.

return

tuple of (select_delta, cursor_delta).
```

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.scroll_down
documentation:
Scroll the dropdown menu down to show more items, also move the selector.

```
param n

scroll down n times. Each time we scroll down SCROLL_SPEED items.

return

tuple of (select_delta, cursor_delta).
```

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.scroll_up
documentation:
Scroll the dropdown menu up to show more items, also move the selector.

```
param n

scroll up n times. Each time we scroll up SCROLL_SPEED items.

return

tuple of (select_delta, cursor_delta).
```

methods.name: zelfred.dropdown.Dropdown.menu
documentation:
The list of items to show in the UI. The list is determined by
`selected_item_index` `cursor_position` and `show_items_limit`
together. It is a subset of `self.items`.

```
return

a list of tuples, each tuple contains an item and a boolean value
indicating whether the item is selected or not.
```

Simulate an items dropdown menu. User can move or scroll selector up and down
to select an item, and then perform {mod}`~zelfred.action`.

For example, the `[x] Beautiful is better than ugly.`,
`[ ] Explicit is better than implicit.` and
the `[ ] Simple is better than complex.` part in the following UI is the
dropdown menu.

```bash
(Query):
[x] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[ ] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[ ] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

```
param items

All items in this dropdown menu. We only show SHOW_ITEMS_LIMIT
items in the UI at a time

param n_items

total number of items, it is a cache of len(items).

param selected_item_index

the selected item index, the value can be larger
than SHOW_ITEMS_LIMIT if we have many items.

param cursor_position

the selected item cursor position in the dropdown UI,
it is a value from 0 to SHOW_ITEMS_LIMIT - 1.

param show_items_limit

max number of items to show in the UI.
```

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e01_random_password_generator
documentation:
Feature:

The user enters the length of the password, and the UI generates a few random passwords
for the user to choose from. The user can tap "Enter" to copy the selected password
to the clipboard, and the UI will exit.

Difficulty: Easy

Dependencies:

```bash
pip install pyperclip>=1.8.0,<2.0.0
```

Demo: <https://asciinema.org/a/617869>
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: zelfred.gallery.e01_random_password_generator.remove_chars
documentation: implement the random password generator

functions.name: zelfred.gallery.e01_random_password_generator.handler
documentation:
The handler is the core of a Zelfred App. It's a user-defined function
that takes the entered query and the UI object as inputs and returns
a list of items to render.

variables.name: zelfred.gallery.e01_random_password_generator.to_remove
documentation: we don't use these chars because they are easily confused

classes.name: zelfred.gallery.e01_random_password_generator.Item

methods.name: zelfred.gallery.e01_random_password_generator.Item.enter_handler
documentation: Copy the content to clipboard.

The default `zf.Item` does not implement any user action handler methods.
To copy the password to the clipboard when the user taps "Enter," we need to
define a custom item class and override the `enter_handler` method."

By default, the UI can perform a "user action" when the following keys are tapped:

- Enter
- Ctrl A
- Ctrl W
- Ctrl U
- Ctrl P

When user taps one of these keys, the UI will call the corresponding handler method
and exit immediately.

modules.name: zelfred.gallery
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e10_refresh_cache_v3
documentation:
Feature:

No matter what user entered, always return a random value between 1 and 100.
And this value is based on cache that won't change while user is typing.
However, we want to provide a way to refresh the value. User can type "!~",
then the value will be refreshed after 1 seconds, and the "!~" will be removed
automatically. During the waiting, it will show a helper text to tell user to wait.

Difficulty: Medium

Dependencies: NA

Demo: https://asciinema.org/a/631335
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: zelfred.gallery.e10_refresh_cache_v3.handler
documentation:
The handler is the core of a Zelfred App. It's a user-defined function
that takes the entered query and the UI object as inputs and returns
a list of items to render.

-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation: Clear the item dropdown menu.

whether an exception is raised.

`need_print_query` flag is set to `True` at the end regardless of
whether an exception is raised.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI._print_items
documentation: Core logic for printing items in the dropdown menu.

documentation:
A wrapper of the core logic for printint items, ensures that the
`need_print_items` and `need_run_handler` flag is set to `True`
at the end regardless of whether an exception is raised.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.process_key_pressed_input
documentation:
Process user keyboard input.

Zelfred default key bindings:

- ⬆: tap `Ctrl + E` or `UP` to move item selection cursor up (previous item).
- ⏫: tap `Ctrl + R` to scroll item selection cursor up.
- ⬇️: tap `Ctrl + D` or `DOWN` to move item selection cursor down (next item).
- ⏬: tap `Ctrl + F` to scroll item selection cursor down.
- ⬅️: tap `LEFT` to move query input cursor to the left.
- ➡️: tap `RIGHT` to move query input cursor to the right.
- ⏪: tap `Alt + LEFT` to move query input cursor to the previous word.
- ⏩: tap `Alt + Right` to move query input cursor to the next word.
- ⏮️: tap `HOME` to move query input cursor to the beginning of the line.
- ⏭️: tap `END` to move query input cursor to the end of the line.
- ◀️: tap `Ctrl + K` to delete the previous word.
- ▶️: tap `Ctrl + L` to delete the next word.
- ↩️: tap `Ctrl + X` to clear the query input.
- ◀️: tap `BACKSPACE` to delete query input backward.
- ▶️: tap `DELETE` to delete query input forward.
- 🔴: tap `Ctrl + C` to quit the app.
- ⤴️: tap `F1` to quit the current sub session and go back to the previous session and recover previous user input.

User defined (customizable) item action:

- 🚀: tap `Enter` to perform custom user action.
- 🚀: tap `Ctrl + A` to perform custom item action.
- 🚀: tap `Ctrl + W` to perform custom item action.
- 🚀: tap `Ctrl + U` to perform custom item action.
- 🚀: tap `Ctrl + P` to perform custom item action.

User defined UI keybinding:

- 🚀: tap `CTRL + T` to perform custom UI action, default is "do nothing.
- 🚀: tap `CTRL + G` to perform custom UI action, default is "do nothing.
- 🚀: tap `CTRL + B` to perform custom UI action, default is "do nothing.
- 🚀: tap `CTRL + N` to perform custom UI action, default is "do nothing.
handle key pressed

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.process_input
documentation:
A wrapper of the core logic for processing input, ensures that the
`need_process_input` flag is set to `True` at the end regardless of
whether an exception is raised.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.move_to_end
documentation: Move the cursor to the next line of the end of the dropdown menu.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.repaint
documentation:
Repaint the UI right after the items is ready. This is useful when you want
to show a message before running the real handler.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.run_sub_session
documentation:
Run a sub session with a new handler. User can tap "F1" to go back to the
previous session. See {meth}`~zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_f1
and the ``except exc.JumpOutSessionError:`` part in {meth}`~zelfred.ui.UI.run_session`
to see how it works.

```
param handler

see 

param initial_query

the initial user input query in the sub session.
it is the start-up text in the user input line editor.
```

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.initialize_loop
documentation:
Process the first loop of a session. This loop starts with a hello message,
and then run the handler, then render the UI.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.main_loop
documentation:
This is the main loop of the UI. It starts with waiting for user input,
and the run the handler, then render the UI.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.jump_out_session_loop
documentation:
This loop is executed after a {class}`~zelfred.exc.JumpOutSessionError` is raised.

It pops up the current session from the queue and use the handler
of previous session to run handler, and then render the UI.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.debug_loop
documentation:
Display error message in the UI and wait for new user input.
New user input may fix the problem or trigger another error.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.run_session
documentation:
Run a "session" in the UI. A "session" has a main loop, and it's handler
to process user input query.

Read {ref}`ui-event-loop`
(or [this link](https://zelfred.readthedocs.io/en/latest/03-UI-Event-Loop/index.html))
for more information.

`except exc.JumpOutSessionError:`

This error will be raised when user
{meth}`press F1 <zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.JumpOutSessionError.process_f1>`
If the current session is a sub session, then it rolls backs the handler
and line editor input to the previous state. If the current session is
the main session, then it will do nothing.

`except exc.KeyboardInterrupt:`

This error will be raised when user press `Ctrl-C`. It will move the
cursor to the end and prepare to exit the UI.

methods.name: zelfred.ui.UI.run
documentation: Run the UI.

Zelfred terminal UI implementation.

```
param handler

the handler is the core of a Zelfred App. It's a user-defined
function that takes the entered query and the UI object as inputs
and returns a list of items to render.

param handler

a callable function that takes a query string as input and
returns a list of items.

param hello_message

sometimes the handler execution for the first time
run takes long. This message will be shown to user to indicate that
the handler is still running, and the UI will show the returned items
once the handler is done.

param capture_error

whether you want to capture the error and show it
in the UI, or you want to raise the error immediately.

param process_input_immediately

the handler will be called immediately
after user input. This is the default behavior.

param process_input_after_query_delay

the handler will be called after
a delay of  seconds after the first user input. For example,
let's say the query delay is 0.3 second. At begin, the user input is empty,
then user entered "a". If then the user entered "bcd" within 0.3 second,
the handler will not be called to give you the latest items. The handler
will be called until the user entered a new key, let's say "e", from
0.4 second after he entered "a".

param query_delay

read above.

param show_items_limit

max number of items to show in the dropdown menu.

param scroll_speed

scroll speed in the dropdown menu.
```

Additional private attributes:

```
param _handler_queue

user may nest session in another session, this LIFO
queue stores the handler functions of the parent sessions, so that
when we exit the inner session and go back to the previous session.

param _line_editor_input_queue

similar to _handler_queue, but stores
the parent sessions' user input queries.
```

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e09_refresh_cache_v2
documentation:
Feature:

No matter what user entered, always return a random value between 1 and 100.
And this value is based on cache that won't change while user is typing.
However, we want to provide a way to refresh the value. User can type "!~",
then the value will be immediately refreshed, and the "!~" will be removed
automatically.

Difficulty: Medium

Dependencies: NA

Return the list of bookmark object for search.

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e02_calculate_file_checksum
documentation:
Feature:

The user enters (or paste) the absolute path of the file, and the UI generates
a few checksum algorithm options, then user can choose one and hit "Enter" to
copy the checksum value of the selected algorithm. The UI will stay and user can
continue to choose another algorithm and hit "Enter" again.

Difficulty: Easy

Dependencies:

```bash
pip install pyperclip>=1.8.0,<2.0.0
```

Demo: <https://asciinema.org/a/617871>
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation:
Copy the checksum of the file to clipboard.

The path of the file is stored in the `self.variables["path"]`.
The checksum algorithm is stored in the `self.variables["algo"]`.

methods.name: zelfred.gallery.e02_calculate_file_checksum.Item.post_enter_handler
documentation:
We would like to keep the UI displayed after the user hits 'Enter'.
Typically, any user input change will trigger the UI to re-render
and then wait for the next user input. The 'UI.wait_next_user_input()'
method allows you to skip the re-rendering and wait for the next user input."

The default `zf.Item` does not implement any user action handler methods.
To copy the password to the clipboard when the user taps "Enter," we need to
define a custom item class and override the `enter_handler` method."

By default, the UI can perform a "user action" when the following keys are tapped:

- Enter
- Ctrl A
- Ctrl W
- Ctrl U
- Ctrl P

When the user taps one of these keys, the UI calls the corresponding
handler method and exits immediately. To modify this behavior, we can
override the `post_enter_handler` method, which is invoked after the
`enter_handler` method."

modules.name: zelfred.gallery.e06_folder_and_file_search
documentation:
Feature:

User can search folder in a root directory, and then tap "Enter" to enter
a sub query session to search file in the selected folder. At the end, user
can tab "Enter" to open the file using the default application. Also, user can
tap "F1" to exit the sub query session and go back to the folder search session.

Difficulty: Hard

Dependencies:

```bash
pip install fuzzywuzzy>=0.18.0,<1.0.0
pip install python-Levenshtein>=0.21.0,<1.0.0
```

Demo: <https://asciinema.org/a/616119>
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

documentation:
This is the handler for the nested sub query (sub query in sub query) session.

Given a path, you have to create a partial function that using the given
 path. The partial function will become the final handler of the sub query.

This handler returns couple of action you can do with the file.

This is the handler for the sub query session.

Given a folder, you have to create a partial function that using the given
 folder. The partial function will become the final handler of the sub query.

Enter a sub query session.

:::{note}
THIS IS A VERY GOOD EXAMPLE OF HOW TO ENTER A SUB QUERY SESSION.

your main UI loop has a handler, sub query session too. So you need
to define a new handler function for the sub query session, and then
use the `ui.run_sub_session()` method to enter the sub session.
You can also use `initial_query` argument to set the start-up
input query to display in the line editor.
:::
Represent a folder in the dropdown menu.

documentation:
Feature:

Use the user input to sort a list of items by fuzzy match similarity.
Allow user to tap "Enter" to copy the content to clipboard.

Dependencies:

```bash
pip install fuzzywuzzy>=0.18.0,<1.0.0
pip install python-Levenshtein>=0.21.0,<1.0.0
pip install pyperclip>=1.8.0,<2.0.0
```

methods.name: zelfred.gallery.e03_select_item_using_fuzzy_match.Item.enter_handler
documentation: Copy the content to clipboard.

The default `zf.Item` does not implement any user action handler methods.
To copy the password to the clipboard when the user taps "Enter," we need to
define a custom item class and override the `enter_handler` method."

By default, the UI can perform a "user action" when the following keys are tapped:

- Enter
- Ctrl A
- Ctrl W
- Ctrl U
- Ctrl P

When user taps one of these keys, the UI will call the corresponding handler method
and exit immediately.

modules.name: zelfred.vendor.pytest_cov_helper
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: zelfred.vendor.pytest_cov_helper.temp_cwd
documentation:
Temporarily set the current working directory (CWD) and automatically
switch back when it's done.

functions.name: zelfred.vendor.pytest_cov_helper.run_cov_test
documentation:
The pytest-cov plugin gives you the coverage for entire project. What if
I want run per-module test independently and get per-module coverage?

This is a simple wrapper around pytest + coverage cli command. Allow you to run
coverage test from Python script and set the code coverage measurement scope.

Usage example:

suppose you have a source code folder structure like this:

```
/dir_git_repo/
/dir_git_repo/my_library
/dir_git_repo/my_library/__init__.py
/dir_git_repo/my_library/module1.py
/dir_git_repo/my_library/module2.py
```

In your module 1 unit test script, you can do this:

```python
from my_library.module1 import func1, func2

def test_func1():
    pass

def test_func2():
    pass

if __name__ == "__main__":
    from fixa.pytest_cov_helper import run_cov_test

    run_cov_test(
        script=__file__,
        module="my_library.module1", # test scope is the module1.py
        root_dir="/path/to/dir_git_repo",
        htmlcov_dir="/path/to/dir_git_repo/htmlcov",
    )
```

In your all modules unit test script, you can do this:

```python
if __name__ == "__main__":
    from fixa.pytest_cov_helper import run_cov_test

    run_cov_test(
        script=__file__,
        module="my_library", # test scope is the my_library/
        root_dir="/path/to/dir_git_repo",
        htmlcov_dir="/path/to/dir_git_repo/htmlcov",
        is_folder=True, # my_library is a folder
    )
```

```
param script

the path to test script

param module

the dot notation to the python module you want to calculate
coverage

param root_dir

the dir to dump coverage results binary file

param htmlcov_dir

the dir to dump HTML output

param preview

whether to open the HTML output in web browser after the test

param is_folder

whether the module is a folder
```

Reference:

- <https://pypi.org/project/pytest-cov/>

Represent an item in the terminal UI. In the example below, the
`[x] Beautiful is better than ugly` and `subtitle 01` is one item.

```bash
(Query):
[x] Beautiful is better than ugly.
      subtitle 01
[ ] Explicit is better than implicit.
      subtitle 02
[ ] Simple is better than complex.
      subtitle 03
```

```
param uid

item unique id. The UI use this to distinguish different items.

param title

first line of the item. It has a checkbox in front of it to
indicate whether it is selected.

param subtitle

second line of the item.

param arg

argument that will be passed to the action.

param autocomplete

the text that will be filled in the input box when
user hits TAB key.

param variables

arbitrary dictionary object, it can be used in the action.
```

modules.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed
documentation: See {class}`UIProcessKeyPressedMixin`.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.cursor_up_and_down
documentation:
A helper method should be called after the cursor moves up or down.

- need_run_handler: False, the user input query is not changed, so we
    don't need to run the handler again.
- need_move_to_end: True, the dropdown menu may change, so we need to
    move the cursor to the end of the line in the next event loop
- need_clear_items: True, the dropdown menu may change, so we need to
    clear the dropdown menu.
- need_clear_query: False, the user input query is not changed, so we
    don't need to clear the user input query.
- need_print_query: False, the user input query is not changed, so we
    don't need to print the user input query.
- need_print_items: True, the dropdown menu may change, so we need to
    print the dropdown menu.
- need_process_input: True, we need to process the user input next time.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.wait_next_user_input
documentation: Don't repaint

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.cursor_left_and_right
documentation:
A helper method should be called after the cursor moves left and right.

- need_run_handler: False, the user input query is not changed, so we
    don't need to run the handler again.
- need_move_to_end: False, the dropdown menu is not changed, so we
    don't need to move the cursor to the end of the line.
- need_clear_items: False, the dropdown menu is not changed, so we
    don't need to clear the dropdown menu.
- need_clear_query: False, the user input query is not changed, so we
    don't need to clear the user input query.
- need_print_query: False, the user input query is not changed, so we
    don't need to print the user input query.
- need_print_items: False, the dropdown menu is not change, so we
    don't need to print the dropdown menu.
- need_process_input: True, we need to process the user input next time.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_up
documentation:
Default behavior:

select the previous item in dropdown menu.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_down
documentation:
Default behavior:

select the next item in dropdown menu.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_e
documentation:
Default behavior:

select the previous item in dropdown menu.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_d
documentation:
Default behavior:

select the next item in dropdown menu.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_r
documentation:
Default behavior:

scroll up the dropdown menu (press UP 5 times).

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_f
documentation:
Default behavior:

scroll down the dropdown menu (press DOWN 5 times).

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_left
documentation:
Default behavior:

move user input cursor to the previous character.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_right
documentation:
Default behavior:

move user input cursor to the next character.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_alt_left
documentation:
Default behavior:

Move cursor to the beginning of previous word.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_alt_right
documentation:
Default behavior:

Move cursor to the beginning of next word.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_home
documentation:
Default behavior:

Move cursor to the beginning of the line.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_end
documentation:
Default behavior:

Move cursor to the end of the line.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.press_backspace
documentation:
Default behavior:

Delete character backwards in the line editor. Also move cursor to the left.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.press_delete
documentation:
Default behavior:

Delete character forwards in the line editor. Also, the cursor stays.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_k

Default behavior:

Delete the previous word.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_l
documentation:
Default behavior:

Delete the next word.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_tab
documentation:
Default behavior:

Auto complete.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_x
documentation:
Default behavior:

Clear the user input.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_c
documentation:
Default behavior:

Keyboard interrupt.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_enter

User action for enter.

documentation:
Default behavior:

User action for CTRL A.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_w

User action for CTRL W.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_u

Default behavior:

User action for CTRL U.

documentation:
Default behavior:

Do nothing. This is reserved for user custom shortcut.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin.process_ctrl_n

Default behavior:

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin._create_key_processor_mapper
documentation:
Create a key processor mapper. So we can map the key to the corresponding
processor.

methods.name: zelfred.ui_process_key_pressed.UIProcessKeyPressedMixin._process_key_pressed_input
documentation:
Try to process the key pressed

```
return

a boolean flag indicating whether the key pressed is processed
```

This class implements the key pressed event processing logics.

**Important**

All methods whose names start with `process_` are abstract methods
that handle specific key press events. They implement the Default behavior:.
Experienced developers can override them to customize the behavior.

modules.name: zelfred.render
documentation: This module implements the render logic of the terminal UI.
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: zelfred.render.Render

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.print_str
documentation:
打印一个字符串, 可以选择是否换行. 并且自动更新 line_number 的值.

```
param str_tpl

string template, 一个字符串模板.

param new_line

如果是 True, 那么会自动换行, 否则不会. 默认不会.

param kwargs

额外的传递给 str_tpl 的参数.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.print_line
documentation:
打印一行, 默认自动换行.

```
param str_tpl

string template, 一个字符串模板.

param new_line

如果是 True, 那么会自动换行, 否则不会. 默认自动换行.

param kwargs

额外的传递给 str_tpl 的参数.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render._force_initial_column
documentation: 用回车符 (注意! 不是换行符) 把光标移动到本行初始位置.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.move_to_start
documentation: 把光标移动到初始位置. 本质上是把光标向上回退移动到第一行, 然后再用回车符把光标移动到本行初始位置.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.move_up
documentation: 把光标移动到初始位置. 本质上是把光标向上回退移动到第一行, 然后再用回车符把光标移动到本行初始位置.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.move_down
documentation: 把光标移动到初始位置. 本质上是把光标向上回退移动到第一行, 然后再用回车符把光标移动到本行初始位置.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.clear_n_lines
documentation: 把光标以上的 n 行清空, 并把光标移动到行首. 常用于清除掉已经打印过的内容.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.clear_all
documentation:
清除所有内容, 并把光标移动到行首.

Clear

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.format_shortcut
documentation:
Add terminal color to a zelfred keyboard shortcut key.

The default color is magenta. You can override this method to use
other colors.

Example:

Tap {magenta}`Enter` to open url
Format important text

documentation: colored text {magenta}`Tab`

documentation: colored text {magenta}`Enter`

documentation: colored text {magenta}`Ctrl A`

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.CTRL_U
documentation: colored text {magenta}`Ctrl U`

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.CTRL_P
documentation: colored text {magenta}`Ctrl P`

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.F1

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.format_highlight
documentation:
Add terminal color to text you want to highlight.

The default color is magenta. You can override this method to use
other colors.

Example:

this is a very {cyan}`Important message`!

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.format_key
documentation:
Add terminal color to key in a key value pair.

The default color is cyan. You can override this method to use
other colors.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.format_value
documentation:
Add terminal color to value in a key value pair.

The default color is yellow. You can override this method to use
other colors.

methods.name: zelfred.render.Render.format_key_value
documentation:
Add terminal color to a key value pair.

Example:

tag {cyan}`environment` = {yellow}`production`

Generic UI render. It can print string, print line, clear line, and move
cursor up and down.

```
param terminal

blessed terminal object.

param line_number

store the current line number of the cursor.
if 0, means it is at the first line.
if 1, means it is at the second line.

param n_lines

store the total number of printed lines in the terminal.
```

classes.name: zelfred.render.UIRender

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.print_line_editor
documentation:
Render the line editor, the `(Query): user query here|` part. And move
the cursor to the beginning of the next line, so it's ready to print the
dropdown menu.

It assumes that there is nothing in the terminal UI before running this.

```
param line_editor

the new  object.

return

text of the line editor.
```
line editor

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.clear_line_editor
documentation:
Clear the line editor (the query input box at the first line).
It doesn't require to move the cursor to the beginning of the line editor
before calling this function, it will handle that automatically.
This function will move the cursor to the beginning of the line editor
at the end.

Before:

```
(Query): user query here|
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
```

Before:

```
|   # <- this line is empty
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.update_line_editor
documentation:
Replace the user input with the new line editor, and move the cursor
to the beginning of the next line, so it's ready to print the
dropdown menu.

```
param line_editor

the new  object.

return

text of the line editor.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.process_title
documentation:
Make sure the title fix the width of the terminal UI.

```
param title

the item title.

param line_width

the max width of the terminal UI, it will truncate
the title and subtitle if they are too long.
```
dropdown

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.process_subtitle
documentation:
Make sure the subtitle fix the width of the terminal UI.

```
param subtitle

the item subtitle.

param line_width

the max width of the terminal UI, it will truncate
the title and subtitle if they are too long.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.print_item
documentation:
Render one item in the dropdown menu. It looks like:

```
[x] item 1 title here
    - item 1 subtitle here
```

```
param item

the  object to render.

param line_width

the max width of the terminal UI, it will truncate
the title and subtitle if they are too long.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.print_dropdown
documentation:
Render all items in the dropdown menu, it looks like:

```
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
```

It assumes that the terminal UI is showing the query line editor and
there's no item in the dropdown menu in the terminal UI before running this.

```
param dropdown

the  object to render.

param line_width

the max width of the terminal UI, it will truncate
the title and subtitle if they are too long.

return

number of items rendered.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.clear_dropdown
documentation:
Before:

```
(Query): user query here|
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
```

Before:

```
(Query): user query here
|
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.update_dropdown
documentation:
Replace the user input with the new line editor, and move the cursor
to the beginning of the next line, so it's ready to print the
dropdown menu.

```
param dropdown

the  object to render.

param line_width

the max width of the terminal UI, it will truncate
the title and subtitle if they are too long.

return

number of items rendered.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.move_cursor_to_line_editor
documentation:
After the {meth}`Dropdown.print_dropdown` is called, the cursor is at the
end of the UI. This method moves the cursor back to the user input box,
so user can keep typing.

Here's an example:

```
(Query): user query here| <- want to move to here
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
| <- cursor is currently here
```

```
return

n_vertical is the number of line to move up, n_horizontal is
the number of character to move right.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.print_ui
documentation:
Render the entire UI, and move the cursor to the right position.

It assumes the terminal has nothing.

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.move_to_end
documentation:
Move the cursor to the end, this method will be used before exit.
Here's an example:

```
(Query): user query here| <- cursor is currently here
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
| <- want to move to here
```

```
param n_items

number of items in the dropdown menu.
```

methods.name: zelfred.render.UIRender.clear_ui
documentation: Clear the entire UI, and move the cursor to the right position.

The Terminal UI Render. It extends the {class}`Render` class and highly
optimized for the `zelfred` terminal UI layout.

See below example, `|` represents the cursor:

```
(Query): user query here|
[x] item 1 title here
      item 1 subtitle here
[ ] item 2 title here
      item 2 subtitle here
[ ] item 3 title here
      item 3 subtitle here
```

The first line `(Query): user query here|` is the user input box, it always
starts with (Query): , and user can enter any text input after that.
The cursor cannot go beyond the : \`\` part.

User can use `Left`, `Right`, `Backspace` and `Delete` keys to edit the
user input box.

Below the first line is the items drop down menu. Each item has two lines.

The first line is the title, it always starts with [x] \`\` or [ ] .
[x] \`\` means the item is selected, [ ] \`\` means the item is not selected.
You can only select one item at a time, and by default the first item is selected.
There always be one item selected.

The second line is the subtitle, it has 2 indent spaces comparing to the title.

User can use the `UP` and `DOWN` keys to navigate the items drop down menu.

The dropdown menu can show up to 10 items at a time, if the dropdown menu
has more than 10 items, user can scroll down to see the rest of the items using
the `DOWN` key. The `CTRL + UP` and `CTRL + DOWN` key can scroll up and down
10 items at a time.
